member.name: Ian Sollom
member.role: MP for St Neots and Mid Cambridgeshire
member.synopsis: Ian Sollom is the Liberal Democrat MP for St Neots and Mid Cambridgeshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Universities and Skills).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Office: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what artificial intelligence services or tools are used by their department; and whether the department has a contract with each provider of those services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office uses artificial intelligence tools and services across a range of functions and purposes, as set out in the response to UIN 73677 and UIN 5862.This includes publicly available AI technologies, internally developed tools, and those procured...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what artificial intelligence services or tools are used by their department; and whether the department has a contract with each provider of those services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what artificial intelligence services or tools are used by their department; and whether the department has a contract with each provider of those services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to adopting artificial intelligence in a way that is transparent, responsible and accountable to the public.The only generative AI tool currently cleared for use within the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office is Micros...

writtenQuestions.heading: Treasury: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what artificial intelligence services or tools are used by their department; and whether the department has a contract with each provider of those services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Artificial intelligence covers a broad range of capabilities, including data analytics, machine learning, automation and newer generative AI tools. These capabilities are widely embedded within modern software and digital services used across government, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what artificial intelligence services or tools are used by their department; and whether the department has a contract with each provider of those services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: AI is embedded across Defence. The Ministry of Defence uses a range of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools, including Microsoft 365 Copilot and Chat‑MOD, to support productivity and business processes. The Department has appropriate contractual and licensi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Engineering and Technology: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to help tackle gender imbalances in pre and post-16 academic and vocational pathways into engineering and technology careers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is compulsory for all pupils to study science at key stage 4 and, since the 2024/25 academic year, it has been compulsory for all pupils to study science at GCSE. Following the Curriculum and Assessment Review, the government is ensuring science is mad...

writtenQuestions.heading: Higher Technical Qualifications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether he has assessed the potential merits of allowing Growth and Skills Levy funding to be used for Higher Technical Qualifications, particularly for adult learners.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is investing over £18 billion per year in post-16 education and skills and is delivering record investment this parliament, committing an additional £1.2 billion a year by the 2028–29 financial year to support around 65,000 more learners a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to publish options for potential changes to the allocation methodology for capital funding for local highway authorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department has not yet set a date for publishing options for potential changes to the highways maintenance block funding formula. The review of the formula will be conducted before 2029/30, the end of the period for which highways maintenance block all...

writtenQuestions.heading: Equitable Life Assurance Society: Compensation

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Equitable Life Payment Scheme has been fully wound down and closed since 2016. The only remaining part of the Payment Scheme in operation is the annual payments made to eligible With-Profit-Annuitants and the Scheme is on track to distribute the remai...

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Construction

writtenQuestions.answerText: MHCLG Ministers and officials engage regularly with the Department for Health and Social Care about infrastructure provision. The new National Planning Policy Framework we recently consulted upon stresses the importance of all forms of public services inf...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The EHRC's draft Code says that service providers should consider whether the impact on those excluded from a separate or single-sex service outweighs the benefits of achieving the legitimate aim.It is for service providers to read the draft Code's guidan...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when her department will begin to engage with local highways authorities ahead of reviewing the highways capital funding formula ahead of the 2030/31 financial year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department engages regularly with local highway authorities and sector bodies to support the maintenance and renewal of the local road network.The review of the funding formula will be conducted to align with the end of the period for which highways m...

writtenQuestions.heading: Equality: Codes of Practice
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what legal advice was sought and received on whether the draft Equality Act 2010 Code of Practice for Services, Public Functions and Associations fulfils the statutory purpose set out in section 14 of the Equa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not comment on legal advice it may have received. We have always been clear that there is a due process that needs to be followed by all. The draft Code was laid in Parliament by SoS on 21 May 2026. A Written Ministerial Statement was ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Music and Dance Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when he plans to announce Music and Dance Scheme funding for the 2026-27 academic year; and what assessment he has made of the potential impact of a lack of announcement on potential funding on schools' ability
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has committed to a continuation of the Music and Dance Scheme. Providers will be informed of future funding shortly.

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what assessment his Department has made of the effectiveness of the Civil Service Pension Scheme MP casework sessions in Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office awarded Capita the contract to administer the Civil Service Pension Scheme in November 2023 under the previous government. The current delays facing scheme members are entirely unacceptable, and this Government has taken firm action to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Kidney Diseases: Health Services

writtenQuestions.answerText: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) and cardiovascular disease (CVD) are closely linked, with shared risk factors, as well as being risk factors for each other. As set out in the 10-Year Health Plan, we will publish a new cardiovascular disease Modern Service Fr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
